_meta.description: Distributed-AI model map for the office simulation (replaces the single-Gemini-engine v0.1.0). Routine per-case agent work (agents 1-4, 5-9, 11) runs on Groq's free tier (agents/workers/groq-client.js callGroq(), agent-base.js queryGemini()) — this is the fix for the recurring Gemini 429s documented in TOKEN-BUDGET.md. Cloudflare Workers AI handles lightweight case routing/classification (callCFRouter()) and is the same-session fallback when Groq is unavailable or Gemini returns 429 (callCloudflareFallback()), via the Worker's native AI binding (wrangler.toml [ai] binding = "AI") — no extra credentials. Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite (per CLAUDE.md 'Launch Decisions' — gemini-2.0-flash is deprecated, do not reintroduce it) is reserved for monthly/quarterly/semi-yearly/yearly report synthesis (meeting-engine.js). The app's AI Search bar always uses Claude (data-center-api). Agent 10 (Architect) never calls an AI for routine cases — see agent-runner.js processCaseBatch()'s Agent-10 special case.

claude_cap_rationale: 30 calls/day = ~$6/month. Distributed across 10 active agents by model_usage_rate. Primary purpose: model training through realistic IT interactions.
description: Groq and CF AI are free — run agents hard. Claude API is paid — cap at 30 calls/day maximum across ALL agents combined, distributed per-agent by model_usage_rate so the model gets meaningful, spread-out training exposure rather than being front-run by whichever agent runs first.
